title: Support multiple deterministic dataset roles per score
description:
## Summary
Extend deterministic dataset planning so a score can use more than one fixed dataset role in the optimization process.

## Deliverables
- Define how a score can distinguish a small fixed regression set from a larger fixed benchmark set.
- Resolve whether these roles are separate score-owned dataset pointers, a role-tagged association model, or an equivalent explicit design.
- Ensure the design supports staged optimization workflows without overloading one dataset to serve incompatible purposes.

## Dependency note
This work extends the score-owned reference dataset assignment and should stay aligned with dataset provenance decisions.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation with explicit score-owned role pointers: keep referenceDataSetId as active pointer and add dedicated deterministic role pointers for regression and benchmark datasets (referenceRegressionDataSetId, referenceBenchmarkDataSetId).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented explicit multi-role deterministic dataset support on Score.

Design choice delivered:
- Separate score-owned dataset pointers (no association model):
  - referenceRegressionDataSetId
  - referenceBenchmarkDataSetId
- Existing referenceDataSetId remains available as the active/general pointer.

Implementation:
- Schema: added role pointers + belongsTo relations on Score in dashboard/amplify/data/resource.ts.
- Dashboard client: extended Score create/update types and update mutation selection in dashboard/utils/amplify-client.ts.
- Python API model: added both fields in plexus/dashboard/api/models/score.py.
- MCP surfaces: added both fields to score info responses in MCP/tools/score/scores.py and MCP/tools/score/management.py.

Validation: python -m py_compile passed for modified Python modules.
